Chronology of every recorded fact about [[Dreadgod]], in reading order. Generated from the same verified fact store as the main article.

== Unsouled ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|'''Ch 11''' — A Dreadgod attack once claimed a quarter of the valley.}}

== Skysworn ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|'''Ch 7''' — A Dreadgod is a potential threat that could kill everyone.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|'''Ch 11''' — The Dreadgod is a major threat that causes bloodspawn to spread.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|'''Ch 11''' — The Dreadgod has servants that can weaken people before it wakes up.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|'''Ch 12''' — The bloodspawn came from a Dreadgod.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|'''Ch 16''' — The Dreadgod's scream had quieted, but Stormrock was still rushing away.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|'''Ch 16''' — The Dreadgod is described as being too bright.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|'''Ch 17''' — The Dreadgod's light makes the Blood Shadow parasite stronger.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|'''Ch 17''' — The Dreadgod emits a bloody light.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|'''Ch 17''' — Yerin can cut bloodspawn off from the Dreadgod.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|'''Ch 17''' — The Dreadgod is an incomprehensibly vast creature that took to the sky.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|'''Ch 17''' — The Dreadgod has a sickle-like beak.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|'''Ch 18''' — Monarchs can temporarily destroy the body of a Dreadgod.}}

== Ghostwater ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|'''Ch 1''' — The Dreadgod's attack occurred in the town Enkai defended.}}

== Underlord ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|'''Ch 4''' — The Dreadgod's rampage broke the wall defending against the Trackless Sea.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|'''Ch 13''' — One Dreadgod has risen early.}}

== Wintersteel ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 1''' — A Dreadgod is the only being within a thousand miles capable of being Adama's opponent.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 2''' — A Dreadgod is a type of entity that can be removed using Penance.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 2''' — A Dreadgod is mentioned as a potential interruption to the tournament.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 3''' — There is a Dreadgod.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 5''' — Mercy warns Pride not to poke any Dreadgods.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 5''' — The Dreadgod has unique madra and divine techniques.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 9''' — The Dreadgod is expected to rise in a few weeks.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 13''' — Abyssal Palace is preparing for the coming of their Dreadgod.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 14''' — The Dreadgod's will can lend strength to an Enforcer technique.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 19''' — The territory was being prepared for the arrival of the Dreadgod.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 24''' — Lindon is gathering a team to go to the Dreadgod.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 24''' — The Dreadgod has a home.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 24''' — The Dreadgod will head for Sacred Valley when it wakes.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 25''' — The Dreadgod is heading toward Lindon's home.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 28''' — The battle around the Dreadgod was becoming too much.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|'''Ch 28''' — The Dreadgod's spiritual pressure crashed over the Valley.}}

== Bloodline ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 2''' — The Dreadgod is in the path of Lindon's homeland.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 2''' — The Dreadgod is currently feeding at Sky’s Edge.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 4''' — A Dreadgod casts its shadow over the landscape.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 4''' — The Dreadgod's glancing blow was capable of destroying a cloudship.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 5''' — The Dreadgod is a being that Orthos might be in the path of.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 6''' — The approach of the Dreadgod affects the earth aura.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 6''' — Yerin wants to track her master's footsteps once a Dreadgod is no longer about to fall on them.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 8''' — A Dreadgod attack is a threat to Lindon's family.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 9''' — A Dreadgod is coming.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 9''' — A Dreadgod is an insane sacred beast as tall as a mountain.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 10''' — A Dreadgod attack is an incoming danger.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 10''' — Dreadgods possess power that overwhelms all aspects of vital aura.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 10''' — The Dreadgod is an incoming threat whose footsteps can be felt in the earth.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 10''' — The characters' immediate plans are to get away from the Dreadgod.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 11''' — A Dreadgod is a monster.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 12''' — There is concern regarding whether the clan can escape before the Dreadgod arrives.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 12''' — The Dreadgod was moving slowly through the mountains.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 13''' — The Dreadgod's approach causes ground shaking.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 13''' — A Dreadgod is something to be cautious of.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 13''' — A Dreadgod's heel can crush people.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 14''' — Dreadgods are unable to retrieve their prize and will eventually forget its location.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 14''' — The Dreadgod is clearing a way.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 15''' — The Dreadgod is a being that can move east and break through mountains.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 15''' — The Dreadgod gives off an impression of overwhelming power.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 15''' — The Akura cloudships are moving away from the Dreadgod.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 17''' — There was a Dreadgod to the west and one to the east.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 17''' — A Dreadgod's attention can cause blood aura to flare in power.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 17''' — The Dreadgod's head snapped up to focus on a new figure.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 17''' — The Dreadgod's techniques are tinged blood-red.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 17''' — The Dreadgod can unleash lightning and sword-madra slashes.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 18''' — Lindon considers fighting a Dreadgod.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 19''' — The Dreadgod's eyes are swirling clouds of every shade of yellow.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 19''' — The Dreadgod has a wrist that was impacted by the Hollow King's Spear.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 19''' — A lance of bright red madra pierced the Dreadgod.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 19''' — The Dreadgod's tail is falling on Lindon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 19''' — A Dreadgod is in Sacred Valley and is subject to physical laws there.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 21''' — A Dreadgod is a powerful entity that Mercy's mother's daughter faced down.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|'''Ch 21''' — The Dreadgod is gone.}}

== Reaper ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 3''' — Yerin mentions that her master did not leave a Dreadgod tucked away.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 4''' — The Blackflame Empire will not miss a Dreadgod's rampage.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 4''' — Fighting a Dreadgod causes exertion.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 4''' — Ziel stated he was not going to fight the Dreadgod.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 5''' — A second Dreadgod appeared.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 6''' — The Dreadgod's rampage left Remnants in Sacred Valley.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 8''' — A loss of power to the suppression field might call the Dreadgod back.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 10''' — Elder Whisper has seen no one enter or leave the labyrinth since the attack of the Dreadgod.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 25''' — Mercy's group did not emerge with a new Dreadgod.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|'''Ch 26''' — The footsteps of a Dreadgod can shake a tiny pond.}}

== Dreadgod ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 3''' — The Dreadgod is a potential entity that might trick Kerani into walking outside.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 3''' — The Dreadgod is close enough to be a disaster.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 3''' — Lindon is not supposed to come near the Dreadgod.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 3''' — The Dreadgod wants slaves more than victory.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 3''' — The Dreadgod is stalking forward.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 3''' — The Dreadgod is referred to as the Silent King.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 3''' — The Dreadgod's new lair is three hundred miles southwest of Lindon's location.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 3''' — The Dreadgod sleeps in a dark cave.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 3''' — Others must be brought to the level of fighting Dreadgods and Monarchs.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 5''' — The Dreadgod mentioned is the least-powerful on its own.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 5''' — The Monarchs must fight against the Dreadgod.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 10''' — Underestimating a Dreadgod can lead to death.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 14''' — Redmoon gathers power to supplement the Dreadgod�s transportation.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 14''' — The Dreadgod's power was around Yerin but its presence was not felt.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 15''' — A powerful spirit broke free with the aid of the Dreadgod.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 16''' — The Dreadgod's will was being wrestled against by some.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 16''' — Lindon dashed toward the Dreadgod.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 18''' — A Dreadgod's power can be held back by stolen power, though not forever.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 18''' — The Dreadgod is the tiger of the Silent Servants cult.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 18''' — The Dreadgod assaulted Lindon's mind.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 19''' — The Dreadgods are currently self-aware.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 21''' — The Dreadgod was shot through the head with Blackflame.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 21''' — Lindon killed the Dreadgod.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 21''' — Lindon intends to carve out the heart of a Dreadgod to make it a weapon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|'''Ch 24''' — Wei Shi Lindon Arelius is considered the fourth Dreadgod.}}

== Waybound ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 3''' — The group has previously faced down a Dreadgod.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 3''' — Lindon's hand/will is compared to a Dreadgod's.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 4''' — The Stormcallers have a Dreadgod.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 5''' — The Weeping Dragon is a Dreadgod.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 5''' — Dreadgods can generate illusions.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 6''' — Lindon is borrowing the power of the Dreadgods to defeat them.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 9''' — The clouds overhead were not signs of a coming Dreadgod.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 10''' — Dreadgods are opponents that cannot be exhausted or outlasted.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 12''' — The others are stuck until they can face a Dreadgod.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 17''' — The Archlords are in a Dreadgod's presence.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 18''' — None of the characters are in a shape to fight a Dreadgod.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 19''' — Lightning snaked out from the Dreadgod and fed on Lindon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 19''' — Dreadgods can affect the aura such that the sky changes colors when they are around.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 19''' — The Dreadgod possesses a furious will.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 21''' — There are two Dreadgods left.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 25''' — Two Dreadgods' wills were used to haul Lindon through space via the horn.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 30''' — The Dreadgod roared.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 30''' — Two Dreadgods died at the same time.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|'''Ch 36''' — The people in the new world might be human-form Dreadgods.}}

== Threshold: Stories from Cradle ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|'''Ch 13''' — Larian refers to Lindon as a Dreadgod.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|'''Ch 14''' — A Dreadgod passed.}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|'''Ch 14''' — The Dreadgod cries out to switch back after the portal closes.}}

[[Category:Timelines]]
